title: Divide without crashing
prompt: Write safe_divide(a, b) that returns a / b, but returns None if b is zero (instead of crashing). Use try/except.

details.restate: Divide a by b and return the result. But if b is zero (which normally crashes the program), catch that and return None instead.

io.because: dividing by zero would crash, so you catch it and return None

terms.meaning: Python's way of saying 'no value / nothing here'

help.plain: Do the division, but wrap it so a divide-by-zero returns None instead of crashing.
help.analogy: A circuit breaker — instead of letting a bad operation blow up the whole program, it trips safely.
help.mnemonic: TRY the risky thing, EXCEPT when it fails.
help.nudge: What error does Python raise on divide-by-zero? Wrap the division so you can catch that.
